_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Determine level of assurance of developed capabilities
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with clear criteria based on organizational maturity, team capability, and long-term operational cost.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Ability AI-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-006 (LS, cosine: 0.538), OP-K-008 (OP, cosine: 0.521).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.3/10 (raw 0.234). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [assurance, capabilities, determine, developed, level] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, based, build, buy, capability, clear] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.853 (P=0.855, R=0.852). Balanced P/R — the 7-word NICE element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Determine level of assurance of developed capabilities" and "Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with c" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.545 > STS 0.234 (gap +0.312). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [assurance, capabilities, determine, developed, level]. WIDAI terms: [ability, based, build, buy, capability, clear].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.312 with low STS 0.234. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
